Big Sur SOD Analysis
  • Project Notes
  • Analysis
  • Data Download

On this page

  • Objectives: quantifying fire/disease impacts
    • Needed libraries
    • Recycled functions
  • Univariate statistics of fire and disease impacts
    • Living Biomass changes
    • CWD Biomass changes
  • Underlying drivers of biomass and stem density changes
    • Mixed effects models
    • Logistic regression of mortality status
  • Tracking stems through time
    • Survival Analysis
    • CWD Decomposition
    • CWD Mixed Modeling

Objectives: quantifying fire/disease impacts

  • Quantify biomass, stem density changes pre to post fire. Compare across burn scar, species, and status.
    • Used mixed effects models to understand underlying relationships.
  • Track stems through time to quantify rates of mortality, snag fall and CWD decomposition.
    • Use survival analysis (mortality), ?? (snag fall), regression (CWD decomp.)

Needed libraries

library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(gtsummary)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(lme4) 
library(lmerTest)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(patchwork)
library(gt)

Recycled functions

read_me <- function(path){
  read_csv(here("data", path))
}
export_image <- function(img, path){
  ggsave(filename = here("exports", path), plot=img, width=12, height=8)
}

climate_theme <- function(color = "slategray1") {
  theme(
  plot.title = element_text(
      size = 16,
      family = "Times New Roman",
      color = "black", 
      margin = margin(b = 15)
    ),
    # add border 1)
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linetype = 1),
    # color background 2)
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = color),
    # modify grid 3)
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y =  element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    # modify text, axis and colour 4) and 5)
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black",  family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(colour = "black", family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
}
# darkolivegreen3

Univariate statistics of fire and disease impacts

Living Biomass changes

biomass_changes <- read_me("./clean/post_fire_summary.csv")
burn_change <- function(burn, time1) {
  df <- biomass_changes |>
    filter(BurnScar == burn) |>
    select(!c(Elevation,Slope,Fire)) |>
    mutate(Pre = case_when(
      SampleYear <= time1 ~ "Pre",
      SampleYear >=  time1 ~ "Post",
      TRUE ~ "NA")) 
  df_mod1 <- df |>
    filter(Pre %in% c("Pre","Post")) |>
    group_by(Plot, Species, BurnScar, PRam, Pre) |>
    summarise(
      TotalStem   = sum(TotalStem, na.rm = TRUE),
      LiveBiomass = sum(LiveBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Pre", values_from=c("TotalStem","LiveBiomass"))
  # only want to evaluate data with both pre and post 
  df_mod2 <- na.omit(df_mod1)
  df_mod3 <- df_mod2 |>
    mutate(ChangeStem = TotalStem_Post - TotalStem_Pre,
      ChangeBiomass = LiveBiomass_Post - LiveBiomass_Pre) |>
    select(c(1,2,3,4,9,10))
  return(df_mod3)
}
# apply summary function to dataset we want
sites_tree <- tibble(
  burn  = c("dolan", "sober", "chalk", "control", "dolan_chalk", "sober_chalk"),
  time1 = c(2020, 2016, 2007, 2016, 2007, 2007)
)
demo_tree <- pmap_df(sites_tree, burn_change)

post_fire_summary_biomass <- demo_tree |>
  group_by(Species, BurnScar, PRam) |>
  summarize( n = n(),
            MeanStemChange = mean(ChangeStem, na.rm=TRUE),
            MeanBiomassChange = mean(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MedianStemChange    = median(ChangeStem, na.rm = TRUE),
            MedianBiomassChange = median(ChangeBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
            SdStemChange = sd(ChangeStem, na.rm=TRUE),
            SdBiomassChange = sd(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE)
) |>
  rename(
         `Mean Change in Stem Density` = 3,
     `Mean Change in Biomass` = 4,
     `Median Change in Stem Density` = 5,
     `Median Change in Biomass` = 6,
  `SD Change in Stem Density` = 7,
  `SD Change in Biomass` = 8)


kable(post_fire_summary_biomass)

den <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=ChangeStem, y= ChangeBiomass, color = Species, linetype = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se =FALSE) +
    climate_theme() 

dis <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeBiomass, color = BurnScar, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme() +
    scale_color_viridis_d()

dis2 <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeStem, color = BurnScar, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()
dis3 <- demo_tree  |>
    ggplot(aes(x=Species, y= ChangeBiomass, color = Species, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()

dis4 <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=Species, y= ChangeStem, color = Species, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()


(dis + dis2) / (dis3 + dis4) / den +
  plot_annotation(title = "Pre to Post Fire Living Tree Summary") +
plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 2))

CWD Biomass changes

cwd_fire <- read_me("./clean/cwd_mass_change_fire.csv")

burn_cwd_change <- function(burn, time1) {
  df <- cwd_fire |>
    filter(BurnScar == burn) |>
    mutate(Time = case_when(
      SampleYear <= time1  ~ "Pre",
      SampleYear >= time1 ~ "Post",
      TRUE ~ "NA")) 
  df_mod1 <- df |>
    filter(Time %in% c("Pre","Post")) |>
    group_by(Plot, BurnScar, Time) |>
    summarise(
      Biomass = sum(PlotCwdMass, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Time", values_from="Biomass",names_expand = TRUE)
  df_mod2 <- df_mod1 |>
    filter(!is.na(Pre) & !is.na(Post))
  df_mod3 <- df_mod2 |>
    mutate(ChangeBiomass = Post - Pre) 
  return(df_mod3)
}

sites_cwd <- tibble(
  burn  = c("dolan", "sober",  "control", "dolan_chalk", "sober_chalk"),
  time1 = c(2020, 2016, 2016, 2009, 2009)
)

cwd_demo <- pmap_df(sites_cwd, burn_cwd_change)

post_fire_cwd_summary <- cwd_demo |>
  group_by(BurnScar) |>
  summarize( n = n(),
            MeanBiomassChange = mean(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MedianBiomassChange = median(ChangeBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
            SdBiomassChange = sd(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MinBiomassChange = min(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MaxBiomassChange = max(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE)
) |>
  rename(`Burn Scar` = 1,
          `Mean Change in Cwd Biomass` = 3,
     `Median Change in Cwd Biomass` = 4,
      `SD Change in Cwd Biomass` = 5,
     `Min Change in Cwd Biomass` = 6,
     `Max Change in Cwd Biomass` = 7) 

kable(post_fire_cwd_summary)

cwd_uni <- cwd_demo |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeBiomass, color = BurnScar)) +
    geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_jitter(show.legend = FALSE) + 
    climate_theme() +
    labs(title= "Pre to Post Fire CWD Changes")

print(cwd_uni)
# anova testing
anov <- aov(ChangeBiomass ~ BurnScar, data = cwd_demo)
print(summary(anov))

Residuals   65 2229646   34302                   
---

print(TukeyHSD(anov))

norm <- cwd_demo |>
  ggplot(aes(x=ChangeBiomass)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  climate_theme() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Response Var")
print(norm)

Underlying drivers of biomass and stem density changes

Mixed effects models

– Multiple regression with mixed effects models iterated across species
- Attempt to understand drivers of biomass changes. Is disease and fire interacting to reduce biomass? Is it even across species.
- Improvements: Run multiple models per species with different explanatory variables, take weighted AIC and weighted effects.
Questions I hope to answer
Which features, topographic or epidemiological, are driving biomass changes in Redwood forests?
Is there a linear relationship between the fixed explanatory variables and biomass?

– P-values for fixed variables below the 0.05 threshold will allow us to reject our null hypothesis and indicate a linear relationship between a fixed explanatory variable and biomass.

Plot biomass changes

  • Scaling values standardizes units? Worth doing?
biomass <-  read_me("./clean/livebiomass_mixedmodel.csv")

species_df <- unique(biomass$Species)

big_biomass_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass + DeadHostBiomass + Elevation + Slope + Fire + PRam +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}

interaction_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass * HostCwdMass * DeadHostBiomass * Elevation * Slope * Fire * PRam +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}


#cleaning function for streamlined results
clean_me <- function(df) {
  aic_lookup <- df |> distinct(Species, AIC)
  df1 <- df |>
    rename(p = 5) |>
    mutate(Results = ifelse(
      p < 0.05,
      paste0("**", round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")**"),
      paste0(round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")")
    )) |>
    select(Species,Term,Results) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Term", values_from="Results") |>
    left_join(aic_lookup, by = "Species")
}

This model uses all potential variables found to date with no interactions.

add_models <- map_df(species_df, big_biomass_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(add_models)
Species (Intercept) PlotCwdMass HostCwdMass DeadHostBiomass Elevation Slope Fire PRam AIC

QUAG -0.187 (5.34e-01) -0.161 (2.38e-01) -0.084 (5.54e-01) -0.092 (6.25e-02) 0.188 (3.17e-01) -0.159 (3.75e-01) -0.229 (3.19e-01) 0.209 (3.11e-01) 169.6953

UMCA 0.182 (6.40e-01) -0.147 (2.27e-01) 0.141 (6.80e-02) 0.017 (7.64e-01) -0.023 (8.72e-01) 0.093 (5.35e-01) -0.255 (2.12e-01) -0.143 (6.82e-01) 290.5294
ARME 0.044 (9.20e-01) 0.434 (2.56e-03) -0.3 (2.81e-02) 0.347 (1.94e-03) -0.065 (6.83e-01) -0.023 (8.90e-01) -0.261 (5.06e-01) 0.173 (5.41e-01) 180.0132

This model uses all potential variables found to date and allows for all possible interactions. Used to find interactions.

int_models <- map_df(species_df, interaction_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(int_models)
Species (Intercept) PlotCwdMass HostCwdMass DeadHostBiomass Elevation Slope Fire PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass PlotCwdMass:Elevation HostCwdMass:Elevation DeadHostBiomass:Elevation PlotCwdMass:Slope HostCwdMass:Slope DeadHostBiomass:Slope Elevation:Slope PlotCwdMass:Fire HostCwdMass:Fire DeadHostBiomass:Fire Elevation:Fire Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:PRam HostCwdMass:PRam DeadHostBiomass:PRam Elevation:PRam Slope:PRam Fire:PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Elevation PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Slope PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope PlotCwdMass:Elevation:Fire HostCwdMass:Elevation:Fire DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Fire PlotCwdMass:Slope:Fire HostCwdMass:Slope:Fire DeadHostBiomass:Slope:Fire Elevation:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:Slope:PRam HostCwdMass:Slope:PRam DeadHostBiomass:Slope:PRam Elevation:Slope:PRam PlotCwdMass:Fire:PRam HostCwdMass:Fire:PRam DeadHostBiomass:Fire:PRam Elevation:Fire:PRam Slope:Fire:PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Elevation:Slope PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Slope HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Slope PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Fire PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Elevation:Fire PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Fire HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Fire PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope:Fire HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:Elevation:Slope:Fire HostCwdMass:Elevation:Slope:Fire DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Elevation:PRam PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:PRam HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Slope:PRam PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope:PRam PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Slope PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Fire PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:HostCwdMass:Elevation:Slope:Fire PlotCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Elevation:Slope:Fire int_me <- function(df,x,y,int) {
  df |>
    ggplot(aes(x={{x}},y={{y}},color=as.factor({{int}}),group=as.factor({{int}}))) +
      geom_point() +
      geom_smooth(method="lm") +
      climate_theme()
}

p1 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, HostCwdMass, PRam)
p2 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, Elevation, PRam)
p3 <- int_me(biomass,Fire, LiveBiomass,PRam)
p4 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, Slope, Fire)


(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)

Other interactions to graph?

This model uses an interaction between HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, Fire and disease.

disease_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass * DeadHostBiomass * Fire * PRam + Elevation * Slope +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}

disease_models <- map_df(species_df, disease_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(disease_models)
Species (Intercept) PlotCwdMass HostCwdMass DeadHostBiomass Fire PRam Elevation Slope HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass HostCwdMass:Fire DeadHostBiomass:Fire HostCwdMass:PRam DeadHostBiomass:PRam Fire:PRam Elevation:Slope HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Fire HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:PRam HostCwdMass:Fire:PRam DeadHostBiomass:Fire:PRam HostCwdMass:DeadHostBiomass:Fire:PRam AIC

ARME 0.467 (8.02e-01) 0.422 (2.75e-02) -0.818 (5.41e-01) 1.281 (6.12e-01) -0.705 (7.07e-01) -0.752 (6.63e-01) -0.004 (9.85e-01) -0.079 (6.93e-01) -1.108 (3.92e-01) 0.59 (6.61e-01) -0.746 (7.67e-01) 0.548 (7.28e-01) -0.674 (7.93e-01) 1.032 (5.61e-01) 0.074 (6.48e-01) 0.832 (5.23e-01) 1.408 (3.01e-01) -0.668 (6.75e-01) 0.19 (9.42e-01) -1.137 (4.10e-01) 194.7337

How do the three models compare in AIC?

model_comp <- bind_rows(
  add_models |> mutate(Model = "Simple Model"),
  int_models |> mutate(Model = "Full Interaction Model"),
  disease_models |> mutate(Model = "Disease/Fire Interaction Model")
) |> 
  select(Model, Species, AIC) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = AIC) |>
  gt()

model_comp
Model LIDE QUAG SESE UMCA ARME
Simple Model 435.6209 169.69533 245.3610 290.5294 180.0132
Full Interaction Model 392.3976 68.92514 104.0875 298.0142 NA
Disease/Fire Interaction Model 425.6804 137.20738 261.9730 306.6452 194.7337

Logistic regression of mortality status

– Multiple logistic regression iterated across species
- Attempting to predict drivers of mortality given binary health status.
- Improvements: Use mixed effects/survival analysis instead of logistic regression.
Questions I hope to answer
Which features, topographic or epidemiological, are driving mortality in Redwood forests?
Is there a relationship between the explanatory variables and mortality?

– P-values for fixed variables below the 0.05 threshold will allow us to reject our null hypothesis and indicate a relationship between the explanatory variables and mortality.

mortality <-  read_me("./clean/mortality_mixedmodel.csv")
species_mortality <- unique(mortality$Species)

mortality_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- mortality |>
    filter(Species == {{species}})
  logistic_model <- glm(Mortality ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass + Elevation + Slope + Fire + PRam,
                    data=df1, family = binomial
                     )
  summary <- summary(logistic_model)
  
  df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
     mutate(Species = species)
  df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
  return(df_summary)
}

mortality_results <- map_df(species_mortality, mortality_model)

mortality_results2 <- mortality_results |>
  rename(p = 4) |>
  mutate(Results = ifelse(
    p < 0.05,
    paste0("**", round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")**"),
    paste0(round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")")
  )) |>
  select(Species,Term,Results) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from="Term", values_from="Results") |>
  select(!`(Intercept)`)

mortality_results3 <- mortality_results2 |>
  rename(Disease = PRam,
        `Plot Cwd Mass` = 2,
        `Host Cwd Mass` = 3)

kable(mortality_results3)
Species Plot Cwd Mass Host Cwd Mass Elevation Slope Fire Disease
Tanoak -0.001 (8.75e-02) -0.016 (2.27e-40) -0.002 (3.38e-56) -0.03 (9.99e-22) 0.677 (2.89e-38) -0.03 (5.04e-01)
Redwood -0.005 (4.98e-14) -0.016 (1.72e-08) -0.001 (4.67e-03) 0.021 (2.39e-04) 0.368 (1.43e-04) -0.289 (2.06e-04)
Bay laurel -0.006 (2.05e-05) 0.001 (6.58e-01) 0 (1.13e-01) -0.052 (2.07e-27) 1.292 (2.15e-40) -0.252 (2.91e-02)

Tracking stems through time

Survival Analysis

1. Need to load and transform data into survival tables

  • Create t and set t0 = 2006
tree <- read_me("./clean/tree_table_FINAL.csv")

tree1 <- tree |>
  filter(Species %in% c("SESE", "LIDE","UMCA")) |> 
  rename(Disease = p_ram.presence,
         Fire = fire_presence) |>
  filter(!is.na(Species)) |>
  mutate(t = SampleYear - 2006,
         Species = case_when(
                             Species == "SESE" ~ "Redwood",
                             Species == "LIDE" ~ "Tanoak",
                             TRUE ~ "Bay laurel")) |>
  select(BS_plot, Species, t,RametFail,Disease,Fire, DBH)

Kaplian - Meier

s1 <- survfit2(Surv(t,RametFail) ~ Species, data=tree1)

s1 |>
  ggsurvfit() +
  facet_wrap(~strata) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title="Kaplan-Meier Survival Function of Big Sur Species", x="Time")
#export_image(p1, "surv.png") 

summary(s1)
Call: survfit(formula = Surv(t, RametFail) ~ Species, data = tree1)

   12  16922    4281    0.534 0.00308       0.5277       0.5398
   13  10297    1131    0.475 0.00320       0.4689       0.4814
   15   8653    2916    0.315 0.00321       0.3088       0.3214
   16   5691    2106    0.198 0.00286       0.1929       0.2041
   17   2588    1845    0.057 0.00195       0.0533       0.0609
survdiff(Surv(t, RametFail) ~ Species, data = tree1)
Call:
survdiff(formula = Surv(t, RametFail) ~ Species, data = tree1)

                       N Observed Expected (O-E)^2/E (O-E)^2/V
Species=Bay laurel 26193     9209    12087   685.475  1249.773
Species=Redwood    20836     8527     8479     0.276     0.442
Species=Tanoak     39700    21158    18328   437.004  1044.174

 Chisq= 1416  on 2 degrees of freedom, p= <2e-16

CWD Decomposition

– Influence of species,disease,fire on decomposition rate of CWD.
– Linearized exponential decay model iterated across the species.
– Aim to estimate values of k and compare between plot conditions.
Questions I hope to answer
How do fire, disease and species impact decomposition rates?
Is there a linear relationship between change in mass and time?

– This piggy backs off earlier research conducted by Dr.Cobb. I am utilizing a known equation for leaf litter decay. Results are simple linear regression, just a lot of build up.
– Improvements: Split the dataset into burned/unburned, run individual models for each

cwd_decay <- read_me("./clean/cwd_decay.csv")
expo_decay <- function(species){
  df <- cwd_decay |>
    filter(Species == species) 
  model <- nls(Y ~ -k*t, data=df, start=list(k=0.7))
  df$PredictedY <- predict(model)
  summary <- summary(model)
  
  return(df)
}
# apply model function to all species
species_vec <- unique(cwd_decay$Species)
expo_results <- map_df(species_vec, expo_decay)
# need to prepare for visuals
expo_results2 <- expo_results |>
  mutate(Fire = as.factor(Fire)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(Burn = ifelse(Fire == 0, "Unburned", "Burned"))


expo_results2 |>
  ggplot(aes(x=t,y=Y, color=Burn)) +
  geom_point(shape=1, size=1.3) +
  geom_line(aes(x=t,y=PredictedY), color="red", linetype="dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~Species,scales = "free") + 
  labs(title="Linearized Exponential Decomposition Models for Studied Big Sur Trees", x="Time (t)", y="", subtitle="ln(Proportion of Mass Change in Year t)",
  color="Fire Status") +
  theme_classic()

CWD Mixed Modeling

Source Code
---
format: 
  html:
    code-tools: true
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true 
    theme: flatly
    page-layout: full
editor: visual
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Objectives: quantifying fire/disease impacts   
- Quantify biomass, stem density changes pre to post fire. Compare across burn scar, species, and status.   
  - Used mixed effects models to understand underlying relationships.   
- Track stems through time to quantify rates of mortality, snag fall and CWD decomposition. 
  - Use survival analysis (mortality), ?? (snag fall), regression (CWD decomp.)    

### Needed libraries   
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(gtsummary)
library(survival)
library(ggsurvfit)
library(lme4) 
library(lmerTest)
library(here)
library(knitr)
library(patchwork)
library(gt)

```    

### Recycled functions  
```{r}
read_me <- function(path){
  read_csv(here("data", path))
}
export_image <- function(img, path){
  ggsave(filename = here("exports", path), plot=img, width=12, height=8)
}

climate_theme <- function(color = "slategray1") {
  theme(
  plot.title = element_text(
      size = 16,
      family = "Times New Roman",
      color = "black", 
      margin = margin(b = 15)
    ),
    # add border 1)
    panel.border = element_rect(colour = "black", fill = NA, linetype = 1),
    # color background 2)
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = color),
    # modify grid 3)
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y =  element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
    # modify text, axis and colour 4) and 5)
    axis.text = element_text(colour = "black",  family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(colour = "black", family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.ticks = element_line(colour = "black"),
    legend.position = "right"
  )
}
# darkolivegreen3

```

## Univariate statistics of fire and disease impacts 

### Living Biomass changes   

```{r}
#| fig-width: 14
#| fig-height: 16


biomass_changes <- read_me("./clean/post_fire_summary.csv")
burn_change <- function(burn, time1) {
  df <- biomass_changes |>
    filter(BurnScar == burn) |>
    select(!c(Elevation,Slope,Fire)) |>
    mutate(Pre = case_when(
      SampleYear <= time1 ~ "Pre",
      SampleYear >=  time1 ~ "Post",
      TRUE ~ "NA")) 
  df_mod1 <- df |>
    filter(Pre %in% c("Pre","Post")) |>
    group_by(Plot, Species, BurnScar, PRam, Pre) |>
    summarise(
      TotalStem   = sum(TotalStem, na.rm = TRUE),
      LiveBiomass = sum(LiveBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Pre", values_from=c("TotalStem","LiveBiomass"))
  # only want to evaluate data with both pre and post 
  df_mod2 <- na.omit(df_mod1)
  df_mod3 <- df_mod2 |>
    mutate(ChangeStem = TotalStem_Post - TotalStem_Pre,
      ChangeBiomass = LiveBiomass_Post - LiveBiomass_Pre) |>
    select(c(1,2,3,4,9,10))
  return(df_mod3)
}
# apply summary function to dataset we want
sites_tree <- tibble(
  burn  = c("dolan", "sober", "chalk", "control", "dolan_chalk", "sober_chalk"),
  time1 = c(2020, 2016, 2007, 2016, 2007, 2007)
)
demo_tree <- pmap_df(sites_tree, burn_change)

post_fire_summary_biomass <- demo_tree |>
  group_by(Species, BurnScar, PRam) |>
  summarize( n = n(),
            MeanStemChange = mean(ChangeStem, na.rm=TRUE),
            MeanBiomassChange = mean(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MedianStemChange    = median(ChangeStem, na.rm = TRUE),
            MedianBiomassChange = median(ChangeBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
            SdStemChange = sd(ChangeStem, na.rm=TRUE),
            SdBiomassChange = sd(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE)
) |>
  rename(
         `Mean Change in Stem Density` = 3,
     `Mean Change in Biomass` = 4,
     `Median Change in Stem Density` = 5,
     `Median Change in Biomass` = 6,
  `SD Change in Stem Density` = 7,
  `SD Change in Biomass` = 8)


kable(post_fire_summary_biomass)


den <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=ChangeStem, y= ChangeBiomass, color = Species, linetype = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_point() +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se =FALSE) +
    climate_theme() 

dis <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeBiomass, color = BurnScar, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme() +
    scale_color_viridis_d()

dis2 <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeStem, color = BurnScar, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()
dis3 <- demo_tree  |>
    ggplot(aes(x=Species, y= ChangeBiomass, color = Species, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()

dis4 <- demo_tree |>
    ggplot(aes(x=Species, y= ChangeStem, color = Species, fill = as.factor(PRam))) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    geom_jitter(shape = 21, 
                position = position_jitterdodge(0.8)) + 
    climate_theme()


(dis + dis2) / (dis3 + dis4) / den +
  plot_annotation(title = "Pre to Post Fire Living Tree Summary") +
plot_layout(heights = c(1, 1, 2)) 


```


### CWD Biomass changes
```{r}
cwd_fire <- read_me("./clean/cwd_mass_change_fire.csv")

burn_cwd_change <- function(burn, time1) {
  df <- cwd_fire |>
    filter(BurnScar == burn) |>
    mutate(Time = case_when(
      SampleYear <= time1  ~ "Pre",
      SampleYear >= time1 ~ "Post",
      TRUE ~ "NA")) 
  df_mod1 <- df |>
    filter(Time %in% c("Pre","Post")) |>
    group_by(Plot, BurnScar, Time) |>
    summarise(
      Biomass = sum(PlotCwdMass, na.rm = TRUE)) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Time", values_from="Biomass",names_expand = TRUE)
  df_mod2 <- df_mod1 |>
    filter(!is.na(Pre) & !is.na(Post))
  df_mod3 <- df_mod2 |>
    mutate(ChangeBiomass = Post - Pre) 
  return(df_mod3)
}

sites_cwd <- tibble(
  burn  = c("dolan", "sober",  "control", "dolan_chalk", "sober_chalk"),
  time1 = c(2020, 2016, 2016, 2009, 2009)
)

cwd_demo <- pmap_df(sites_cwd, burn_cwd_change)

post_fire_cwd_summary <- cwd_demo |>
  group_by(BurnScar) |>
  summarize( n = n(),
            MeanBiomassChange = mean(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MedianBiomassChange = median(ChangeBiomass, na.rm = TRUE),
            SdBiomassChange = sd(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MinBiomassChange = min(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE),
            MaxBiomassChange = max(ChangeBiomass, na.rm=TRUE)
) |>
  rename(`Burn Scar` = 1,
          `Mean Change in Cwd Biomass` = 3,
     `Median Change in Cwd Biomass` = 4,
      `SD Change in Cwd Biomass` = 5,
     `Min Change in Cwd Biomass` = 6,
     `Max Change in Cwd Biomass` = 7) 

kable(post_fire_cwd_summary)

cwd_uni <- cwd_demo |>
    ggplot(aes(x=BurnScar, y= ChangeBiomass, color = BurnScar)) +
    geom_boxplot(show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_jitter(show.legend = FALSE) + 
    climate_theme() +
    labs(title= "Pre to Post Fire CWD Changes")

print(cwd_uni)
# anova testing
anov <- aov(ChangeBiomass ~ BurnScar, data = cwd_demo)
print(summary(anov))
print(TukeyHSD(anov))
norm <- cwd_demo |>
  ggplot(aes(x=ChangeBiomass)) +
  geom_histogram() +
  climate_theme() +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Response Var")
print(norm)


```



## Underlying drivers of biomass and stem density changes   

### Mixed effects models   
-- Multiple regression with mixed effects models iterated across species     
    - Attempt to understand drivers of biomass changes. Is disease and fire interacting to reduce biomass? Is it even across species.       
- Improvements: Run multiple models per species with different explanatory variables, take weighted AIC and weighted effects.  
**Questions I hope to answer**  
Which features, topographic or epidemiological, are driving biomass changes in Redwood forests?  
Is there a linear relationship between the fixed explanatory variables and biomass?  

-- P-values for fixed variables below the 0.05 threshold will allow us to reject our null hypothesis and indicate a linear relationship between a fixed explanatory variable and biomass.



#### Plot biomass changes 

- Scaling values standardizes units? Worth doing? 

```{r}
biomass <-  read_me("./clean/livebiomass_mixedmodel.csv")

species_df <- unique(biomass$Species)

big_biomass_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass + DeadHostBiomass + Elevation + Slope + Fire + PRam +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}

interaction_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass * HostCwdMass * DeadHostBiomass * Elevation * Slope * Fire * PRam +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}


#cleaning function for streamlined results
clean_me <- function(df) {
  aic_lookup <- df |> distinct(Species, AIC)
  df1 <- df |>
    rename(p = 5) |>
    mutate(Results = ifelse(
      p < 0.05,
      paste0("**", round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")**"),
      paste0(round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")")
    )) |>
    select(Species,Term,Results) |>
    pivot_wider(names_from="Term", values_from="Results") |>
    left_join(aic_lookup, by = "Species")
}


```

**This model uses all potential variables found to date with no interactions.**   

```{r}
add_models <- map_df(species_df, big_biomass_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(add_models) 

```


**This model uses all potential variables found to date and allows for all possible interactions. Used to find interactions.**       

```{r}
int_models <- map_df(species_df, interaction_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(int_models) 
```

```{r}
#| fig-width: 12 
#| fig-height: 14

int_me <- function(df,x,y,int) {
  df |>
    ggplot(aes(x={{x}},y={{y}},color=as.factor({{int}}),group=as.factor({{int}}))) +
      geom_point() +
      geom_smooth(method="lm") +
      climate_theme()
}

p1 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, HostCwdMass, PRam)
p2 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, Elevation, PRam)
p3 <- int_me(biomass,Fire, LiveBiomass,PRam)
p4 <- int_me(biomass,DeadHostBiomass, Slope, Fire)


(p1 + p2) / (p3 + p4)


```

**Other interactions to graph?**    

**This model uses an interaction between HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, Fire and disease.**       

```{r}
disease_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- biomass |>
    filter(Species == {{species}}) |>
    mutate(across(c(PlotCwdMass, HostCwdMass, DeadHostBiomass, 
                  Elevation, Slope,LiveBiomass), scale))
  tryCatch({
    m1 <- lmer(LiveBiomass ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass * DeadHostBiomass * Fire * PRam + Elevation * Slope +
                    (1|SampleYear) + (1|Plot),
                    data=df1
                     ) 
    summary <- summary(m1)
    df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
      mutate(Species = species,
              AIC = AIC(m1))
    df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
    return(df_summary)
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("Error", e$message)
    return(NULL)
  })
}

disease_models <- map_df(species_df, disease_model) |>
  clean_me()
kable(disease_models) 

```

**How do the three models compare in AIC?**

```{r}
model_comp <- bind_rows(
  add_models |> mutate(Model = "Simple Model"),
  int_models |> mutate(Model = "Full Interaction Model"),
  disease_models |> mutate(Model = "Disease/Fire Interaction Model")
) |> 
  select(Model, Species, AIC) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = Species, values_from = AIC) |>
  gt()

model_comp

```







### Logistic regression of mortality status  
-- Multiple logistic regression iterated across species   
    - Attempting to predict drivers of mortality given binary health status.  
- Improvements: Use mixed effects/survival analysis instead of logistic regression.   
**Questions I hope to answer**  
Which features, topographic or epidemiological, are driving mortality in Redwood forests?  
Is there a relationship between the explanatory variables and mortality?    

-- P-values for fixed variables below the 0.05 threshold will allow us to reject our null hypothesis and indicate a relationship between the explanatory variables and mortality.

```{r}
mortality <-  read_me("./clean/mortality_mixedmodel.csv")
species_mortality <- unique(mortality$Species)

mortality_model <- function(species){
  df1 <- mortality |>
    filter(Species == {{species}})
  logistic_model <- glm(Mortality ~ PlotCwdMass + HostCwdMass + Elevation + Slope + Fire + PRam,
                    data=df1, family = binomial
                     )
  summary <- summary(logistic_model)
  
  df_summary <- as.data.frame(summary$coefficients) |>
     mutate(Species = species)
  df_summary$Term = rownames(df_summary)
  return(df_summary)
}

mortality_results <- map_df(species_mortality, mortality_model)

mortality_results2 <- mortality_results |>
  rename(p = 4) |>
  mutate(Results = ifelse(
    p < 0.05,
    paste0("**", round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")**"),
    paste0(round(Estimate, 3), " (", formatC(p, format = "e", digits = 2), ")")
  )) |>
  select(Species,Term,Results) |>
  pivot_wider(names_from="Term", values_from="Results") |>
  select(!`(Intercept)`)

mortality_results3 <- mortality_results2 |>
  rename(Disease = PRam,
        `Plot Cwd Mass` = 2,
        `Host Cwd Mass` = 3)

kable(mortality_results3)

```

## Tracking stems through time   

### Survival Analysis

#### 1. Need to load and transform data into survival tables  
  - Create t and set t0 = 2006

```{r}
tree <- read_me("./clean/tree_table_FINAL.csv")

tree1 <- tree |>
  filter(Species %in% c("SESE", "LIDE","UMCA")) |> 
  rename(Disease = p_ram.presence,
         Fire = fire_presence) |>
  filter(!is.na(Species)) |>
  mutate(t = SampleYear - 2006,
         Species = case_when(
                             Species == "SESE" ~ "Redwood",
                             Species == "LIDE" ~ "Tanoak",
                             TRUE ~ "Bay laurel")) |>
  select(BS_plot, Species, t,RametFail,Disease,Fire, DBH)

```

#### [Kaplian - Meier](https://www.emilyzabor.com/survival-analysis-in-r.html#part-4-advanced-topics)  

```{r}
s1 <- survfit2(Surv(t,RametFail) ~ Species, data=tree1)

s1 |>
  ggsurvfit() +
  facet_wrap(~strata) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(title="Kaplan-Meier Survival Function of Big Sur Species", x="Time")

#export_image(p1, "surv.png") 

summary(s1)
survdiff(Surv(t, RametFail) ~ Species, data = tree1)

``` 



### CWD Decomposition   
-- Influence of species,disease,fire on decomposition rate of CWD.    
-- Linearized exponential decay model iterated across the species.    
-- Aim to estimate values of k and compare between plot conditions.   
**Questions I hope to answer**  
How do fire, disease and species impact decomposition rates?   
Is there a linear relationship between change in mass and time?     

-- This piggy backs off earlier research conducted by Dr.Cobb. I am utilizing a known equation for [leaf litter decay](https://cdnsciencepub.com/doi/10.1139/x06-012). Results are simple linear regression, just a lot of build up.    
-- Improvements: Split the dataset into burned/unburned, run individual models for each


```{r}
cwd_decay <- read_me("./clean/cwd_decay.csv")
expo_decay <- function(species){
  df <- cwd_decay |>
    filter(Species == species) 
  model <- nls(Y ~ -k*t, data=df, start=list(k=0.7))
  df$PredictedY <- predict(model)
  summary <- summary(model)
  
  return(df)
}
# apply model function to all species
species_vec <- unique(cwd_decay$Species)
expo_results <- map_df(species_vec, expo_decay)
# need to prepare for visuals
expo_results2 <- expo_results |>
  mutate(Fire = as.factor(Fire)) |>
  drop_na() |>
  mutate(Burn = ifelse(Fire == 0, "Unburned", "Burned"))


expo_results2 |>
  ggplot(aes(x=t,y=Y, color=Burn)) +
  geom_point(shape=1, size=1.3) +
  geom_line(aes(x=t,y=PredictedY), color="red", linetype="dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~Species,scales = "free") + 
  labs(title="Linearized Exponential Decomposition Models for Studied Big Sur Trees", x="Time (t)", y="", subtitle="ln(Proportion of Mass Change in Year t)",
  color="Fire Status") +
  theme_classic()

```

### CWD Mixed Modeling  

```{r}
```
